markdownRemark.html:
Checking rows of table data is usually used to implement batch operations or control whether data can be selected, which is especially suitable for scenarios in mid- and back-end systems where large amounts of data need to be handled flexibly. In the Choerodon UI table component, developers can easily achieve different selection interactions through simple configuration, helping to improve user operation efficiency.

Basic Introduction

The selection mode and behavior of table rows are jointly controlled by the data source and component properties, with data layer configuration taking precedence over component rendering layer.

  • Data source configuration controls the data layer, for example: whether a record can be selected or disabled.
  • Component property configuration controls the rendering layer of the selection column, such as selectable mode and custom rendering.

Use Cases

These can be divided into selectable and non-selectable scenarios.

  1. Selectable: Select data rows as needed for relevant logical operations.
  2. Non-selectable: No need for row selection operations, simply display the table.

Selectable

Configure Single/Multiple Selection

Associate with the data source to control single/multiple selection at the data layer by configuring the DataSet: selection: 'multiple' | 'single', default is 'multiple'.

  • selection: 'multiple' | 'single':

    • Record can be selected, linking the relevant selectable component checkbox in multiple or single selection mode.
    • Supports controlling selection attributes and methods on DataSet records, handling data for multiple/single selection.

selection: 'multiple'

selection: 'single'

Conditional Selection Options

Control whether a record is selectable or selected by default based on data or business logic.

Note: Disabled (disabled) and selected (selected) states are controlled independently.

The above table is configured through data:

  1. First row

    1. Not disabled, editable
    2. Selected by default
    3. Selection cannot be controlled

  2. Third row

    1. Editing disabled
    2. Selection can be controlled
...
record: {
  dynamicProps: {
       selectable: (record) => record.get('userid') !== '0',
      defaultSelected: (record) => record.get('userid') === '0',
      disabled: (record) => record.get('userid') === '2',
  },
},
...

Selection Mode Control

Once a record is selectable, how it is selected, interacted with, and custom rendered is controlled by component properties.

Table record selection mode property: selectionMode

  • The default selection mode is rowbox. To avoid accidental actions, selection is triggered only when the checkbox area is clicked.
  • Optional values: rowbox | treebox | click | dblclick | mousedown | none, default value rowbox

1. rowbox Selection Mode, Checkbox Always Visible

To avoid accidental operations, only clicking on the checkbox area triggers the selection action.

2. click | dblclick | mousedown Selection Modes

Selection actions can be quickly performed with the mouse according to user needs.

Note: In dblclick mode, checkboxes are always visible. In click | mousedown selection modes, the table does not show checkboxes by default but can be customized with other properties.

  • dblclick mode, double-click a row for quick selection:
  • click | mousedown mode, click to quickly select, enable selectedHighLightRow to highlight selected rows:
  • click | mousedown mode, click to quickly select, enable alwaysShowRowBox to keep checkboxes always visible:

3. treebox Selection Mode

In a tree table, checkboxes are displayed. Checking a record has a cascading effect. Non-tree tables do not display checkboxes.

4. none Selection Mode, Hide Checkboxes

Other Checkbox Properties

rowBoxPlacement Position of the row selection checkbox; you can set the checkbox column as the first column (start), last column (end), or other column order (number)
selectionBoxRenderer Checkbox renderer; allows customizing the rendering of the checkbox component. Can return null to hide the checkbox for specific rows that do not meet conditions
useMouseBatchChoose Enable or disable mouse batch selection, enabled by default. In rowbox multi-select mode, you can select multiple rows by dragging the mouse. Press at the starting rowbox and release at the ending position.

Non-Selectable

  1. Records that cannot be selected at the data level, configure the DataSet with selection: false,

    • selection: false:
    • Records cannot be selected, and associated component checkboxes are hidden (Table, Tree ...)
    • Selection-related properties and methods in the DataSet are disabled (ds.selected, ds.select() ...)
    • Selection-related properties in the component are also disabled (selectionBoxRenderer ...)

  2. Control only at the component rendering level and cannot be selected, configure Table with selectionMode="none"

    • Component checkboxes are hidden
    • DataSet records related selection properties and methods are available
    • Component-level selection-related properties are unavailable
